name: Lamb Stock- Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: 2 racks of Lamb bones and trimmings
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. In a roasting pan, roast bones and trimmings until well browned, about 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Transfer bones and trimmings to a stockpot and degrease the roasting pan. Place over medium-high heat and stir in 1/2 cup water, scraping up any browned bits. Pour these deglazed juices into the stockpot.
4. Add remaining ingredients and enough water to cover. Bring to a simmer over medium heat, reduce heat to low, cover partially, and simmer for 3 to 4 hours, skimming frequently.
5. Strain stock through a colander lined with double layer of dampened cheesecloth into a bowl. Gently press solids to extract all liquid, then discard the solids. Cool. Remove fat from the surface.
6. Stock can be stored in the refrigerator for up to a week.
